title: Audit Event v1
description:
Canonical schema for a single line in findings/.agent-audit.jsonl.

Every tool invocation against a target is recorded as one JSON object per
line (JSONL). All writers MUST go through lib/labutil.audit() so the entry
is produced with file locking (fcntl.flock), symlink protection, the `agent`
field populated from the environment, and json.dumps (never string
formatting) to prevent JSON injection.

Required fields: ts, agent, action.
Optional fields: target, engagement, exit, detail.
Per-writer extras (e.g. challenge, label, type, name) are allowed via the
canonical writer's **extra channel — see lib/labutil.py:audit().

This schema is a documentation artifact. Runtime enforcement (validating
writes against this schema before they hit disk) is a future task.

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the event was recorded, e.g. 2026-07-19T12:34:56Z. Produced by labutil.audit() as datetime.now(UTC).strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ').

agent.description: Identity of the agent/user that produced the event. Populated from os.environ.get('USER', os.environ.get('LOGNAME', 'agent')).

action.description: What the agent did. Canonical short string, e.g. 'scope-check', 'ctf-evidence', 'nuclei', 'h1-report-check'. Replaces the deprecated, unsafe `cmd` string field.

target.description: Target host or URL the action was performed against. Omitted when the action is not target-specific (e.g. an internal 'issue' report).

engagement.description: Engagement name the action belongs to (matches engagements/<name>.yaml). Empty string means no engagement (global check only).

exit.description: Exit code of the tool invocation. Omitted when the action is not a subprocess invocation.

detail.description: Free-form short human-readable detail. Must not contain secrets, private keys, tokens, or credentials (see h1-report secret-scrubbing test for the pattern).
